name: global_equilibration
title: Global pattern equilibration
question: Does a rectangular brick tiling relax into a rounded, topologically-equilibrated aggregate at T=5?
hypothesis: After 400 MCS the tiling rounds into a disk with <n>~6.04, mu2~0.69, a stable shared initial condition.
objective: Equilibrate a ~1000-cell brick tiling at T=5 and measure total boundary length, moments and light-Medium fraction to confirm a stable equilibrated IC.
description: Reproduction of the Fig 4+ example from Glazier & Graner (Phys. Rev. E 47, 2128, 1993). Exact parameters: J_ll=2, J_dd=2, J_ld=2, J_lM=8, J_dM=8 (gamma_ld=+0, gamma_lM=+7, gamma_dM=+7); T=5.0, lambda=1.0, target A_l=40.0, A_d=40.0. Initial condition: brick_equilibrate. Figures reproduced: Fig 4, Fig 5.
claim: The tiling rounds into a 1012-cell disk with <n>~6.0, mu2~0.69, matching Fig 4b; statistics stable after ~400 MCS (Fig 5).
expected_behavior: Aggregate rounds from rectangle to disk within 400 MCS

embed_visualizations.name: All paper figures
embed_visualizations.title: Global pattern equilibration — full figure set

findings.statement: The tiling rounds into a 1012-cell disk with <n>~6.0, mu2~0.69, matching Fig 4b; statistics stable after ~400 MCS (Fig 5).

conclusion: Reproduced the paper's Global pattern equilibration example with the exact energies and initial condition; the pbg-cpm CPM engine yields the same qualitative behaviour and topology. Figures: Fig 4, Fig 5.
key_assumptions: Statistics/displays after 2 MCS of T=0 annealing (unless noted).

readouts.name: Total boundary length
readouts.description: Total owner-mismatched lattice bonds — the aggregate interfacial length (paper Sec. II D 1).

readouts.name: Fractional boundary lengths
readouts.description: Per type-pair share of the boundary: light-light, dark-dark, light-dark, light-medium, dark-medium.

readouts.name: Mean number of sides
readouts.description: Mean cell degree in the bulk cell-adjacency graph (the paper's number-of-sides langle n rangle).

readouts.name: Topological moments
readouts.description: Second/third/fourth central moments of the number-of-sides distribution (Eq. 6).

derived.insight: The tiling rounds into a 1012-cell disk with <n>~6.0, mu2~0.69, matching Fig 4b; statistics stable after ~400 MCS (Fig 5).
